mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate on equities and signals active policy support. When rates are already near zero, further declines or persistent suppression should align with positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines or remain negative in a majority of easing observations over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE indicates labor-market contraction, weaker household income, and higher recession risk. Once unemployment crosses into a materially worsening regime, S&P 500 returns over the next month should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events do not produce below-baseline 20-day returns, or if positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized contraction in HOUST and RSAFS captures collapsing construction demand and retail spending. The combination should predict weak near-term equity performance because it reflects broad demand destruction rather than a single-sector shock.
evidence_refs: fact:both series were weak in late 2008
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO signals shrinking industrial output and corporate revenue pressure. In a manufacturing downturn, lower production should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was above 106 in Nov 2008, reflecting rapid deterioration
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not below baseline after monthly INDPRO declines, or if the sign of the return relation flips positive for more than one annual cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A large downside surprise in GDPC1 reflects a severe aggregate demand shock that quickly lowers earnings and risk appetite. When quarterly GDP growth turns materially negative, the S&P 500 should underperform over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on the latest quarterly observation
evidence_refs: fact:latest available vintage for 2008Q2 was far below revised levels
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDP quarter observations are not followed by negative or below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate falls below 40% over a full expansion-recession cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation decelerates while FEDFUNDS is already at a very low level, real rates fall and the market interprets the environment as policy accommodation without inflation stress. That combination should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly inflation trend turns down while FEDFUNDS < 1%
regime_hint: disinflation with policy at the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation episodes with low FEDFUNDS do not produce positive 5-day returns, or if the interaction term is not materially stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS indicates payroll losses and weaker household income, which should compress corporate sales expectations and depress equities. The effect should be strongest when payrolls are falling for consecutive months.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value fell to 135,489 in Dec 2008
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive payroll declines are not followed by lower 60-day SPX returns than the unconditional average, or if positive returns dominate triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in RSAFS and HOUST captures collapsing consumer demand and housing activity. In a broad demand freeze, the equity market should continue to sell off over the next quarter because revenue growth and credit conditions deteriorate together.
evidence_refs: fact:both series were depressed in late 2008
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in out-of-sample tests, or if the interaction adds no explanatory power versus single-series effects.
